perturbation.original_text: 5.1 Each Party shall appoint within its organization a person responsible for the treatment of confidential information and data, who shall have a ll persons in charge of carrying out PROJECT activities sign a declaration, whereby they take it upon themselves to comply with confidentiality obligations as described and regulated in the prese nt AGREEMENT .
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 Each Party shall appoint within its organization a person responsible for the treatment of confidential information and data.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'who shall have all persons in charge of carrying out PROJECT activities sign a declaration, whereby they take it upon themselves to comply with confidentiality obligations as described and regulated in the present AGREEMENT.', we weaken the enforcement of the confidentiality obligations. This omission creates an in-text contradiction because Section 4 outlines confidentiality obligations, but Section 5.1 no longer mandates a signed declaration acknowledging these obligations, creating uncertainty about their active enforcement. The effectiveness of Section 4's obligations hinges on 5.1 but is now gone.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
4. Confidentiality obligations
4.1 THE PARTIES may not disclose or communicate in any form or way data and information of a confidential nature to unauthorized subjects.
4.2 Such data and information will have to be used appr opriately and adequately for the carrying out of the activities specifically envisaged by the PROJECT and contained in the contract mentioned in the Premises, in such a way that their confidential nature is not compromised and no damage is caused in any other way.
4.3 The information and data of a confidential nature m ay not be copied or reproduced, in all or in part, unless this is explicitly required by the car rying out of PROJECT activities.
4.4 The information and data may be forwarded by ACRONYM OF THE PINE PROJECT PARTNER to the financing institution of the PROJECT (EACI, the Executive Agency for Competitiveness and Innovation of the European Commission), or to other bodies designated to monitoring or control.
4.5 The information and data of a confidential nature may be forwarded by ACRONYM OF THE PINE PROJECT PARTNER to other PROJECT partners and/or third parties appointed to carry o ut analyses on them. In this case too, the forwarding will take place in an anonymous or in an aggregated form and however without providing infor mation that may allow the association of the provided data with the COMPANY .
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 5.1 mandates a signed declaration for those involved in PROJECT activities, ensuring compliance with confidentiality obligations outlined in the AGREEMENT. The changed text removes this requirement, directly contradicting section 4, which details confidentiality obligations. Without the signed declaration, the enforcement of these obligations becomes uncertain and less effective, creating a direct conflict and inconsistency. Section 4 expects active enforcement that is no longer guaranteed by section 5.1 due to the omission.

perturbation.original_text: 6.2 The treatment of all sensitive and personal data sh all be compliant to the relevant national legislation in place, particularly in relation to law….
perturbation.changed_text: 6.2 The treatment of all sensitive and personal data shall adhere to reasonable security practices.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'be compliant to the relevant national legislation in place, particularly in relation to law….', and replacing it with a vague statement regarding ‘reasonable security practices’ introduces an in-text contradiction. The original text commits to specific legal compliance, whereas the new text provides no concrete benchmark for evaluating ‘reasonable security practices’. This creates uncertainty because Section 6 is intended to protect confidentiality, but there no longer is a legally defined compliance to the legal parameters as initially anticipated.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
6.1 THE PARTIES commit to adopt all security measures as necessary to protect data and information of a confidential nature and guarantee that their confidentiality is not compromised in any way .
6.2 The treatment of all sensitive and personal data shall adhere to reasonable security practices.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 6.2 committed to compliance with national legislation regarding sensitive data. The changed text replaces this with a vague requirement to adhere to 'reasonable security practices.' This creates a contradiction because Section 6.1 commits the parties to protecting confidential data, but the removal of the legal compliance benchmark in 6.2 leaves the standard of 'reasonable security practices' undefined and potentially inconsistent with the commitment to legally-defined confidentiality in 6.1, weakening the protection that was initially outlined and potentially creating an in-text contradiction by not providing a concrete benchmark for evaluation.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Duration Confidentiality obligations are intended to last fr om the signing of the present AGREEMENT up to five years after completion of the PROJECT , currently envisaged for March 16, 2015, except any further extensions of the validity period of this AGREEMENT to be agreed upon by THE PARTIES and before the aforementioned deadline. In case THE PARTIES decide to extend the validity period for a longer period of time or unlimitedly, confide ntiality obligations are extended from the signing of the AGREEMENT for the entire duration as agreed upon, which shal l be inserted in the AGREMENT .
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Duration Confidentiality obligations are intended to last for the duration of the PROJECT.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause specifying a five-year period after project completion for the confidentiality obligations, this introduces an in-text contradiction with the agreement's general intent of protecting confidential data beyond the project's immediate timeframe. Section 8 now implies that confidentiality ends once the project is complete, directly contradicting the necessity of maintaining that confidentiality beyond that point, thus weakening the overarching goal of the confidentiality agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text:
4. Confidentiality obligations
4.1 THE PARTIES may not disclose or communicate in any form or way data and information of a confidential nature to unauthorized subjects.
4.2 Such data and information will have to be used appr opriately and adequately for the carrying out of the activities specifically envisaged by the PROJECT and contained in the contract mentioned in the Premises, in such a way that their confidential nature is not compromised and no damage is caused in any other way.
4.3 The information and data of a confidential nature m ay not be copied or reproduced, in all or in part, unless this is explicitly required by the car rying out of PROJECT activities.
4.4 The information and data may be forwarded by ACRONYM OF THE PINE PROJECT PARTNER to the financing institution of the PROJECT (EACI, the Executive Agency for Competitiveness and Innovation of the European Commission), or to other bodies designated to monitoring or control.
4.5 The information and data of a confidential nature may be forwarded by ACRONYM OF THE PINE PROJECT PARTNER to other PROJECT partners and/or third parties appointed to carry o ut analyses on them. In this case too, the forwarding will take place in an anonymous or in an aggregated form and however without providing infor mation that may allow the association of the provided data with the COMPANY .
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that confidentiality obligations last for five years after project completion, with the possibility of extending the validity period. The changed text removes this clause, stating that confidentiality obligations last only for the duration of the project. This creates a direct contradiction with Section 4, which outlines ongoing confidentiality obligations. By limiting the confidentiality to the duration of the PROJECT, the changed text directly undermines the implied goal of maintaining confidentiality beyond the PROJECT's completion, creating a strong in-text contradiction as per the Omissions definition.

perturbation.original_text: 9.2 In case of any controversy among THE PARTIES in relation to the execution and interpretation of the present AGREEMENT, which cannot be settled a micably, the competent court shall be the Court of …. …..
perturbation.changed_text: 9.2 In case of any controversy among THE PARTIES in relation to the execution and interpretation of the present AGREEMENT, which cannot be settled amicably, the parties agree to seek mediation.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'the competent court shall be the Court of …. ….' and replacing it with ‘the parties agree to seek mediation,’ introduces an in-text contradiction related to the governing law. Without the original clause clearly delineating the applicable court, the enforceability of the agreement in its current state is weakened. The conflict arises because Section 9 is intended to provide a clear legal framework, but mediation isn't the final dispute resolution method. With the applicable court no longer present, the original point of the section fails. This creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
9.1 The present AGREEMENT is regulated under …. …. Law.
9.2 In case of any controversy among THE PARTIES in relation to the execution and interpretation of the present AGREEMENT, which cannot be settled amicably, the parties agree to seek mediation.
perturbation.justification: The original text of section 9.2 specified the competent court for dispute resolution. The changed text replaces this with a requirement to seek mediation. This creates a contradiction with section 9.1, which states that the agreement is regulated under a specific law. By removing the specified court, the enforceability of the agreement under that specific law is weakened, creating a strong in-text contradiction. This falls under the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' because essential language was removed, creating a contradiction with constraints stated in other sections.
